page_title: Central University of Kashmir

text: the year 2009 by an act of parliament. later the university was bifurcated into two universities : the central university of kashmir ( cuk ) and the central university of jammu ( cuj ). establishment of the university : the state government earmarked 503 acres of land at tulmulla and its adjoining areas in the ganderbal district for the establishment of the permanent campus of the university. in may 2009, the administrative office of the university was set up at baghi - hyder, hyderpora, srinagar. the university later shifted its operation to sonwar, srinagar. with the expansion of the academic & administrative activities, the university established three ( 03 ) campuses at nowgam area of srinagar. in 2018, the university shifted to ganderbal and is currently functioning from the following campuses : main campus tulmulla, ganderbal green campus, duderhama, ganderbal science campus, nunar, ganderbal arts campus, beehama, ganderbal the main campus of the university at tulmulla is spread over a vast landscape with scenic surroundings, providing the campus with a calm ambiance suited for an academic institution. the construction work is currently going on in full swing at the campus site. the university is gradually shifting its departments to the new campus at tulmulla. academics & administration : currently, the university has nine ( 09 ) schools of study with 21 constituent teaching departments offering 40 + research, pg, integrated, ug, diploma, and vocational programmes. the academic affairs of the university are conducted under the dean academic affairs. the university has an effective and transparent examination system that adheres strictly to the academic calendar, despite all odds, while transacting its routine business. the examination and evaluation wing ( eew ) of the university conducts examinations for all the programmes of study in a time - bound manner. to encourage quality research and streamline all research related activities, the university has established research and development cell
page_title: Central University of Kashmir

text: academic affairs. the university has an effective and transparent examination system that adheres strictly to the academic calendar, despite all odds, while transacting its routine business. the examination and evaluation wing ( eew ) of the university conducts examinations for all the programmes of study in a time - bound manner. to encourage quality research and streamline all research related activities, the university has established research and development cell. the university has a full - fledged office of dean students welfare ( dsw ), an active central placement cell ( cpc ), and a robust / active directorate of quality assurance ( diqa ). the university has also established directorate of information & support system ( ditss ) that provides it - related services to the scholars, students, faculty, and staff of the university. browsing centers on all the campuses have been established with 24x7 high bandwidth internet services. the university has also embarked on an ambitious e - governance project for the automation of academic and administration processes. cuk ’ s journey : throughout the difficult circumstances in kashmir, the university has maintained high standards in academics & research. it is a moment of pride for the cuk family that the university in such a short span of time has grown considerably not only in terms of human resources but also in terms of infrastructure. after operating from rented accommodations, the university is finally settling down in campuses at ganderbal, the home district of cu kashmir. we vividly see ourselves achieving and progressing while surmounting the hurdles. the central university of kashmir will continue to make a mark in terms of academic excellence and it keep attracting students, scholars, and teachers from across the country. read more : academic & administrative reforms in cu kashmir ( 100 days report 2023 ) cu kashmir ’ s 10 year journey central university of kashmir green campus, ganderbal, j & k - page_title: Central University of Kashmir

text: formula of hindi, english and kashmiri ( national, international and local ) and also the internationalization of higher education as envisaged in nep - 2020. the ‘ inner circular frame ’ of the logo symbolizes the ‘ inclusive policy of the university ’. the ‘ ribbon base ’ on which the inner circular frame is resting depicts the ‘ strong scholarly values ’ on which the foundation of the university will be laid. the leaf at the top end of the circle is a ‘ chinar leaf ’ that defines the ‘ unique cultural and geographical identity of the state ’. the chinar leaf symbolizes ‘ longevity, strength, refreshment and source of life ’. the inception of the leaf also signifies the ‘ environmental concern and friendliness ’ that this university will strive for in future. the ‘ olive branches ’ are a symbol of ‘ peace, goodwill and reconciliation ’. the use of olive branch as a symbol of peace dates back to the 5th century b. c. the 30 pinnate sessile leaves, 15 on each side represent 30 weeks of an academic calendar. the ‘ book ’ in the university logo symbolizes the main function of the university i. e ‘ creation and dissemination of knowledge ’. the ‘ blue color ’ of the inner rings stand for ‘ scholarly intent ’ ; ‘ white background ’ stands for ‘ cleanliness ’ ; ‘ green color ’ of leaf stands for ‘ youth ’. the number of pinnate sessile leaves is 15, on each side, representing 30 weeks of an academic calendar. the colors have been taken from saffron flower that grows abundantly in kashmir in the country and is a royal spice with huge international demand. the colors of saffron flower aptly define the objectives and aspirations of this university with purple color depicting royalty, nobility, luxury, power, aspiration, creativity, wisdom, dignity, grandeur, devotion, peace and pride. orange color representing optimism, confidence, enthusiasm, warmth, and agreeableness. red color
page_title: Central University of Kashmir

text: in kashmir in the country and is a royal spice with huge international demand. the colors of saffron flower aptly define the objectives and aspirations of this university with purple color depicting royalty, nobility, luxury, power, aspiration, creativity, wisdom, dignity, grandeur, devotion, peace and pride. orange color representing optimism, confidence, enthusiasm, warmth, and agreeableness. red color is symbolizing passion, love and desire to achieve academic excellence. the tulip flower in the outer circle symbolizes for fame and fortune and also represents internationalization. the yellow color depicts positive feelings. joy, happiness, and hope. it also boosts confidence and curiosity. the sixteen diamonds in outer rim of the logo represent the 16 schools. central university of kashmir green campus, ganderbal, j & k - 191201 https : / / www. cukashmir. ac. in last updated : 03 / 04 / 2026 13 : 01 ist documents university act university ordinances university regulations cadre reqruitment rules annual reports annual accounts self study reports ( ssr ) empanelled doctors academic calendar 2025 - 26 empanelled hospitals holidays 2025 university wall calendar 2025 national and international days statutory bodies important links national academic depository academic bank of credits student grievance redressal samarth portal ( administration ) samarth portal ( students ) samarth portal ( administration ) online payments portal grievance portal cuk e - office viksit bharat flag code of india initiatives internal complaints committee chief vigilance office anti ragging cell equal opportunity cell ( eoc ) rti cell publications cuk law review journal of research & innovation cukashmir newsletter supports : firefox 2. 0 + google chrome 6. 0 + safari 4. 0 + edge terms disclaimer privacy policy copyright policy hyperlink policy sitemap help docs © central university of kashmir | developed by : umar bashir, directorate of itss | version 4.

text: : to disseminate and advance knowledge by providing instructional and research facilities in such branches of learning as the university may deem fit. to make special provisions for integrated courses in humanities, social sciences, science, and technology in its educational programs. to take appropriate measures for promoting innovations in the teaching - learning process and inter - disciplinary studies and research. to educate and train manpower for the development of the country. to establish linkages with industries for the promotion of science and technology, and, to pay special attention to the improvement of the social and economic conditions and welfare of the people, their intellectual, academic, and cultural development. central university of kashmir green campus, ganderbal, j & k - text: ##i home menu pages dean academics affairs prof. ( dr ) shahid rasool dean academic affairs | shahidrasool @ cukashmir. ac. in green campus, central university of kashmir, duderhama, ganderbal - 191201 dean academic affairs message professor, convergent journalism, dean, school of media studies, director, media and public relations centre, central university of kashmir, ganderbal, kashmir, j & k. formerly : us fulbright fellow, syracuse university, new york director, cemca, commonwealth of learning, canada. director, emrc, university of kashmir media advisor, university of kashmir state coordinator, for uts of j & k and ladakh ( for 70 th year celebrations of indian constitution ) head, media education research centre, uok. significant academic achievements us fulbright fellow in communication technology ; ms in communication, syracuse university, usa. ; ph. d. masters in mass communication and journalism, university of kashmir, srinagar 32 years of experience in teaching, research, production, and direction of educational films / e - content and multimedia. guided research at m. phil. & phd level at the university of kashmir. examiner for evaluation of phd thesis and paper setter for entrance examinations. authored 16 research papers / policy reports / articles, one book, produced and directed over 300 etv films including 5 national level award winning films. chief editor of publications journal media times, weekly merc times and cemca newsletter. chief producer weekly quest – video digest on dd srinagar and weekly kurd – radio digest, air, srinagar. co - pi for development of e - contents in 6 ug subjects - 850 e - content / multimedia modules and over 1000 learning objects. development of moocs for cec / mhrd under nme - ict project. member mhrd standing committee for econtent development and dth ( swayam and swayam prabha ). delivered lectures / keynote speeches at various
page_title: Central University of Kashmir

text: ##nagar. co - pi for development of e - contents in 6 ug subjects - 850 e - content / multimedia modules and over 1000 learning objects. development of moocs for cec / mhrd under nme - ict project. member mhrd standing committee for econtent development and dth ( swayam and swayam prabha ). delivered lectures / keynote speeches at various places in the country and presented papers in various national / international conferences in india and abroad. organised over 20 workshops / film festivals / orientation programmes etc. provided consultancy to universities / colleges to launch ug / pg courses in mass communication and develop curriculum and infrastructure for the same.. also guided emrc ’ s across country, create infrastructure and develop programmes. member of various academic bodies at national level. the most recent is member ugc ’ s high powered academic advisory council on moocs ; expert on ugc sap programme at ccmg, jmi university and madras university ; naac subcommittee for developing a tool for assessment and accreditation of odl institutions ; and also served as member on various selection committees for academic positions in various universities in the country. served as a member / chairperson on bos / drc / academic council and various committees at the university of kashmir. was instrumental to launch regular research programme at merc, university of kashmir administrative experience director, cemca ( a un diplomatic mission in new delhi for 8 commonwealth asian countries ), commonwealth of learning, vancouver canada ( from june 2016 - may 2019 ) director emrc, university of kashmir from june 2007 to may 2016 and june 2019 to july 2020 ( plus two years as in - charge 2005 - 07 ) hod, merc, university of kashmir – april 2008 to january 2012. academic coordinator sit – virtual classroom at ku. executive director, for 2 year for bbc - wst project for promoting social affairs reporting. chairman / member – media advisory committee / central purchase committee etc. at ku for over 15 years. chairman
page_title: Central University of Kashmir

text: 2019 to july 2020 ( plus two years as in - charge 2005 - 07 ) hod, merc, university of kashmir – april 2008 to january 2012. academic coordinator sit – virtual classroom at ku. executive director, for 2 year for bbc - wst project for promoting social affairs reporting. chairman / member – media advisory committee / central purchase committee etc. at ku for over 15 years. chairman cec / ugc committee for revision of staffing pattern of cec and mcs and chairman dpc cec. member board of management of various media centres. member governing council / governing board / technical advisory committee, finance committee, coordination committee of cec / ugc. member on ugc committee for sanction and establishment of new media centres in india member on development of guidelines for outsourcing e - contents at cec / ugc member of standing committee for development of e - content and dth channels at mhrd. member on ugc ’ s high power academic advisory council, to consider and approve the course coordinators and moocs proposals my mission : “ to light candles of knowledge everywhere ” central university of kashmir green campus, ganderbal, j & k - 191201 https : / / www. cukashmir. ac. in last updated : 03 / 04 / 2026 13 : 01 ist documents university act university ordinances university regulations cadre reqruitment rules annual reports annual accounts self study reports ( ssr ) empanelled doctors academic calendar 2025 - 26 empanelled hospitals holidays 2025 university wall calendar 2025 national and international days statutory bodies important links national academic depository academic bank of credits student grievance redressal samarth portal ( administration ) samarth portal ( students ) samarth portal ( administration ) online payments portal grievance portal cuk e - office viksit bharat flag code of india initiatives internal complaints committee chief vigilance office anti ragging cell equal opportunity cell ( eoc )

text: ##derbal district. the campus was shifted from srinagar and established in the erstwhile cmo office, ganderbal in 2018. the campus is situated about 25 kilometres from srinagar ’ s lal chowk city centre and 01 kilometre from the green campus of the university at duderhama ganderbal. located at a stone ' s throw from the dc office ganderbal, the arts campus is situated at a location full of hustle - bustle and a lively environment. currently, hundreds of students are pursuing various degrees on the campus. if you are a student interested in arts, literature, languages and other related fields, the arts campus is your abode. following academic departments are located in the arts campus : department of english department of urdu department of kashmiri how to reach the arts campus : if you are reaching us via land route : the following routes can be followed : lal chowk ( srinagar ) > dalgate > khanyar > soura > nagbal > behama > arts campus lal chowk ( srinagar ) > karan nagar > eidgah > > soura > nagbal > behama > arts campus contact details of director arts campus, beehama ganderbal : name designation email phone number central university of kashmir green campus, ganderbal, j & k - 191201 https : / / www. cukashmir. ac. in last updated : 03 / 04 / 2026 13 : 01 ist documents university act university ordinances university regulations cadre reqruitment rules annual reports annual accounts self study reports ( ssr ) empanelled doctors academic calendar 2025 - 26 empanelled hospitals holidays 2025 university wall calendar 2025 national and international days statutory bodies important links national academic depository academic bank of credits student grievance redressal samarth portal ( administration ) samarth portal ( students ) samarth portal ( administration ) online payments portal grievance portal cuk e -

text: around 20 kilometers from lal - chowk, srinagar. dotted with majestic chinar trees, the green camps rests on the banks of river sindh, presenting a picturesque environment. the presence of heritage chinars in the green campus presents a sight of a well preserved ecological and heritage campus. the green campus was established at duderhama in 2019 at the erstwhile physical college of education, ganderbal. the green campus is home to various academic & administrative departments which gives it a mixed presence of people from the administration and young students donning uniforms. green campus of the university also hosts the directorate of it & ss that provides seamless internet, intranet, web and other it services to the university students, scholars, faculty and staff. in addition, the green campus hosts the following administrative & academic departments : academic departments : department of management studies department of tourism studies department of politics & governance department of commerce department of economics department of religious studies administrative sections : academics section examination & evaluation wing ( eew ) directorate of itss ( ditss ) research & development cell office of dean students welfare ( dsw ) estates & stores section public relations office ( pro ) hospitality & protocol transport section rajbhasha cell how to reach the green campus : if you are reaching us via land route : the following routes can be followed : lal chowk ( srinagar ) > dalgate > khanyar > soura > nagbal > behama > duderhama > green campus. lal chowk ( srinagar ) > karan nagar > eidgah > > soura > nagbal > behama > duderhama > green campus. central university of kashmir green campus, ganderbal, j & k - 191201 https : / / www. cukashmir. ac. in last updated : 03 / 04 / 2026 13 : 01 ist documents university act university ordinances university regulations cadre reqruitment rules annual reports annual accounts self study

text: campus at tulmulla, ganderbal : the central university of kashmir is located at ganderbal district of union territory of jammu & kashmir. around 30 kilometres from srinagar ’ s city centre lal - chowk, the main campus of the central university of kashmir is located at tulmulla, ganderbal. the campus is spread over a vast landscape with scenic surroundings, providing the campus a calm ambience suited for an academic institution. the central university has acquired 503 acres of land in the tulmulla area of ganderbal to create a state of the art campus that will fulfil all the requirements of a modern university. the university has build several academic & administrative buildings at the main campus site, tulmulla. the construction work is currently going on in full swing at the campus site. the university is gradually shifting more departments to its new campus at tulmulla. being the main site for university ' s construction work, the main campus is home to engineering wing of the university that overlooks the construction works carried out by various agencies. the campus hosts the data centre of the university housing the major networking, intranet, communication and surveillance equipment. besides, the campus also hosts the following academic & administrative departments : academic departments at main campus tulmulla : department of education department of information technology department of communication & journalism department of mathematics department of physics department of physical education department of law department of civil engineering administrative sections : vice chancellor ’ s secretariat registrar office finance section accounts section administration section purchases section recruitment section how to reach the main campus tulmulla : if you are reaching us via land route : the following routes can be followed : lal - chowk ( srinagar ) > khanyar > soura > nagbal > behama > duderhama > tulmulla. lal - chowk ( srinagar ) > karan nagar > eidgah > > soura > nagbal > behama > duderhama >

text: urdu kashmiri home menu pages science campus about cuk science campus : the science campus of the central university kashmir is situated at nunar in ganderbal district. this campus is about 24 kilometres from srinagar ’ s lal - chowk city centre and 14 kilometres from the main campus tulmulla. the science campus is located less than 6 kilometres from the green campus of the university. after operating from outside the ganderbal district for years, the university shifted science departments to the erstwhile district institute of education and training ( diet ) building at nuner, ganderbal in 2018. like the other campuses of the university at ganderbal, the decision by the university to move to the location was to realise the dream of establishing all the campuses in central university ’ s home district of ganderbal. the new location has facilitated the department in providing a separate base for growing as a hub of scientific research and development. currently, students from major science subjects like zoology, botany, biotechnology and chemistry are pursuing their higher studies in the campus. the science campus at nunar is equipped with high speed internet, smart class rooms, 24 / 7 cctv surveillance and other facilities. various science labs have also been established at the campus. following academic departments are located at the science campus : department of biotechnology. department of zoology. department of botany. department of chemistry. how to reach the science campus : if you are reaching us via land route : the following routes can be followed : lal chowk ( srinagar ) > dalgate > khanyar > soura > nagbal > behama > science campus nuner. lal chowk ( srinagar ) > karan nagar > eidgah > > soura > nagbal > behama > science campus nuner. contact details of director science campus, nuner ganderbal : name designation email phone number prof. ( dr. ) abid hamid dar professor & dean ( school of life sciences ) ab

text: 1 regulation 01 meetings of the executive council ( section 2 & statutes 38 ( 1 ) of the central universities act, 2009 ) ( approved by the executive council at its first meeting held on 03. 04. 2010 ) 1. these regulations may be called “ regulations for the conduct of meetings of the executive council ” and shall come into force on the date they are notified by the university. 2. the date for a meeting of the executive council shall be fixed by the vice - chancellor, who is the chairman of the executive council. 3. the notice for a meeting of the executive council shall be issued by the registrar. 4. ordinarily, a notice in writing of 21 days shall be given for a meeting of the executive council. provided that the vice – chancellor in case of urgency may convene an urgent meeting of the executive council by giving a shorter notice whenever he thinks it necessary. 5. the registrar, shall circulate, at least 7 days before the meeting, the notes on the items of the agenda, as approved by the vice – chancellor to be considered at a meeting. provided that it shall be open to the chairman, to bring up such items which are urgent and important in nature for consideration at the meeting itself. 6. a special meeting of the executive council may be convened by the registrar, if a request to this effect is received in writing from not less than 50 % of the total number of members of the executive council on a day to be fixed in consultation with the vice - chancellor. members requis itioning such a spec ial meeting should indicate the agenda item ( s ), which they propose to discuss at the meeting and the special meeting shall consider only those items. provided that a special meeting shall not be held unless all the member s who requisitioned such meetings are present. provided further that the requisition shall be deemed to have been cancelled if there is no quorum at such a meeting. 7. quorum
page_title: cukapi disgenweb in p upload 1664789857006 CUK 20Regulations pdf

text: indicate the agenda item ( s ), which they propose to discuss at the meeting and the special meeting shall consider only those items. provided that a special meeting shall not be held unless all the member s who requisitioned such meetings are present. provided further that the requisition shall be deemed to have been cancelled if there is no quorum at such a meeting. 7. quorum for a meeting of the executive co uncil shall be as laid down in the relevant statutes of the university act *. 8. where a meeting of the executive council has been duly convened and no quorum is present within half an hour of the ti me appointed for the meeting, the meeting will be adjourned to the same day or any ti me in the next week or to such other day and at such other time and place, as the members of the executive council may decide, a notice for the adjourned meet ing shall be sent to all the members of the executive council. if no quorum is present at an adjourned meeting within half an hour of the appointed time, th e members present shall constitute the quorum. 9. the conduct of business at a meeting of the executive council shall be regulated by the chairman. 10. ordinarily all decisions shall be by consensus. however, if circumstances so warrant, the chairman may put a resolution to vote and the decision shall be carried by a majority. in case of a tie, the chairman shall have a casting vote as well. 11. where a matter is to be considered by the executive council it shall be open to the vice - chancellor to obtain the approval of the members of the council by circulation. in that event, the draft reso lution shall be circulated together with explanatory notes and also the copies of the papers and documents connected therewith and the draft resolution shall be deemed to have been approved, if it is signed by a majority of the members of executive council. 12. the

text: departments of central university of kashmir. strengths • central university of kashmir has been established as a pace setting institution along with other 14 central universities. as such the focus in the university is on adopting multidisciplinary approach to create new knowledge with a social purpose. the curriculum designing, instruction and evaluation in this university has undergone a significant change and has set a departure from the traditional routine and rhetoric curriculum designing, teaching and examination. this policy shift is a major strength. in fact, the subject experts on our own boards of studies and school boards from leading institutions at the national level including central universities, iit ’ s, iim ’ s, national law universities, ncte, ncert, have significantly contributed in this direction. • our teaching - learning process is completely ict - enabled and student centric. we are following an innovation practice of curriculum transaction strategy ( cts ) wherein a detailed break - up of the syllabus at the micro level is provided to the learners at the beginning of each semester. the cts contains the schedule regarding the number of lectures, number of ppts, number of tutorials, discussions for each semester together with unit wise coverage through core - readings. • strength with us is our student - teacher ratio of 10 : 1. this ratio is helping us in paying individual attention to each student in the class catering to diverse needs of learners. • the other important feature is our effort to cater to the diversities by enhancing inclusive character of our own classrooms : 50 % of our students belong to sc / st, other backward classes ( obc ) & pwd categories. weaknesses • the university till date could not develop its own campus owing to the poor quality of land allotted by the state govt. in 2010. the construction of the university buildings at the allotted site ( tulmulla, ganderbal ) could not materialise during the last five years. as a result of this, the university started operating from multiple rented campuses in srinagar. as an

text: ##wd categories. weaknesses • the university till date could not develop its own campus owing to the poor quality of land allotted by the state govt. in 2010. the construction of the university buildings at the allotted site ( tulmulla, ganderbal ) could not materialise during the last five years. as a result of this, the university started operating from multiple rented campuses in srinagar. as an alternative, in the first instance, the university now has decided to construct pre - engineered buildings which are expected to be completed within the next four months. • the university could not yet appoint faculty at the level of professors and associate professors. 72 % of the professors positions and 92 % of associate professors positions are still lying vacant because of non portability of pension. • a number of academic programmes especially those which require establishment of laboratories with the state of art facilities could not be established due to rental nature of the existing campuses. • several programmes aimed at fostering social responsibility and community development could not take off. • no organised sports activity for students could be managed due to lack of a full - fledged sports ground at the campus. • dearth of supportive staff is also one of the major issues which hamper the speedy disposal of the cases pertaining to administration, academics, research etc. opportunities • the university has a great scope for becoming a pace - setting institution in the entire northern region of the country. • mou with three northern central universities shall facilitate the following : • credit transfer in all common programmes ; text: of social sciences, mumbai. 45. list the teaching methods adopted by the faculty for different programmes : the department of politics & governance appreciates the value of effective curriculum transaction strategies and even an excellent curriculum will not be fruitful if there are no appropriate transaction strategies. we believe that the main responsibility for curriculum transaction lies on the teaching faculty who are encouraged to use innovative and varied types of pedagogies to foster a learning environment for the students where intellectual understanding and critical thinking are nurtured. a broad range of teaching techniques are employed by our teaching faculty to achieve these goals. apart from the traditional lecture method, we also utilize several complementary techniques like seminar ( small group teaching ), tutorials, research seminar, exercise classes, workshops ( classroom based practical classes ), problem - solving sessions, work based practice and fieldwork. information technology aids are used extensively to facilitate these techniques wherever necessary. in order to assess the effectiveness of our curriculum transaction strategies, continuous assessment is done through evaluation and through student feedback. our assessment methods include not only tests and exams, but we also encourage exhibitions, performances, presentations, simulations, multimedia projects, and a variety of other written, oral, and visual methods to evaluate learning outcomes. 46 : no change 47. highlight the participation of students and faculty in extension activities : prof. noor ahmad baba paper presentations and workshops ( 2010 - text: icc, internal complaints committee ( icc ) : six years of commitment and progress the central university of kashmir is committed to provide i ts employees, scholars and students a safe and conducive environment to work in. in order to create and maintain an environment in the u niversity that is gender sensitive and free from harassment, the u niversity constituted internal complaints committee ( icc ) against sexual harassment in 2014 following the guidelines on prevention and deterrence of sexual harassment at workplace as laid down by the sexual harrasment of women at workplace ( prevention, prohibition and redressal ) act, 2013. the committee was first headed by ms. gulafroz jan, assistant professor, department of law, who was the founding presiding officer. the committee was reconstituted in 2015 with ms. insha farooq, 4 | p a g e assistant professor, department of management studies as the presiding officer. later, ms. ishrat bashir, assistant professor, department of english served as presiding officer in charge from september 2015 to may 2016 as ms. insha farooq proceeded on leave. prof. nighat basu took over as presiding officer of icc in july 2017 to 2019. professor parveen pandit took over as the presiding officer in 2019 till date icc presiding officers s. no name tenure year 1. ms. gulafroz jan one year 2014 2. ms. insha farooq one year june 2015 - aug. 2015 ; june 2016 - june 2017 3. ms. ishrat bashir eight months sep. 2015 - may 2016 4. prof. nighat basu - - 2017 - 2019 4 prof. parveen pandit 2019 till date mandate the mandate of the committee is : prevention and deterrence of sexual harassment in the university. implementation of the zero tolerance policy of the university against the sexual harassment in the university. 5 | p a g e creating conducive and safe environment fo r the

text: “ gender equality – issues, concerns and prospects ” on 28th march, 2016 in collaboration with dsw and iqac, central university of kashmir. the seminar was divided into two sessions. in the first session, a lecture was delivered on gender equality by prominent social activist mrs. nighat shafi pandit. she spoke about patriarchy, gender violence and other issues related to gender inequality in our society. she emphasized the role of educational institutions in creating a gender sensitive society. 07 01 02 9 | p a g e in t he second session, a panel discussion was held on many forms of gender inequalities in our society and the role of icc in creating healthy workplaces free from sexual harassment of women. panelists included prof. lily want, department of english, university of kashmir, prof. parveen pandit, principal, government college of education, srinagar, prof. shafeeqa parveen, former direct or, dde, university of kashmir, dr. yasmeen ashai, principal, government degree college for women and noor mohammad baba, head, department of politics governance, 10 | p a g e central university of kashmir. the panel discussion was moderated by dr. gulafroz jan, assistant professor, department of legal studies. the panel discussed how gender inequality i s propagated in the society and how could we work towards a gender just society. around 200 participants from various departments of the university attended the seminar. there was active participation in the open house discussion and participants were appreciative 11 | p a g e about getting an opportunity to know and discuss gender sensitivity and implementation of the sexual harassment of women at workplace ( prevention, prohibition and redresssal ), act 2013. interactive sessions icc conducted numerous interactive sessions with various departments of the university. during each interactive session, a powerpoint presentation was given that explained in detail : what constitutes sexual harassment? who are protected under sexual harassment of women in workplace act?

text: ##structural facilities. icc urgently needs additional space and support staff to carry out its day to day activities. hesitation and bias regarding icc in some sections of the community is due to the larger societal context where sexual harassment is attached to honour / dishonour. such views lack gender sensitivity and icc has to address these concerns. 19 | p a g e gender segregated sensitisation is one of the short comings of icc. the intersectionality of religion, g ender, class, caste and region creates a barrier in the minds of many facilitating such segregation. one of the most important challenges in front of icc is to break these barriers. frequently asked questions 1. do we have sexual harassment ‘ here ’? 2. why do we need icc? 3. why is institutional support required in case of sexual harassment? 4. doesn ’ t talking about sexual harassment actually increase the number of such cases? 5. is talking about sexual harassment permissible in ‘ our society and culture ’? 6. does icc exist simply to punish men? 7. what does icc about the issue of interference by administration? 8. is icc actually an independent institution? 9. who come under the purview of icc? 10. can icc implement its recommendations on its own? 11. how does icc identify what is a genuine case? 12. is icc making the university ‘ too sensitive ’? 20 | p a g e plans for future holding more gender sensitisation activities such as interactive sessions, workshops, seminars, pamphlet distribution, debates, poster making etc. organizing a capacity building programme in collaboration with women studies centre, ku and sign an mou with the centre. organizing orientation programmes for icc members. organizing a workshop on convention on the elimination of all forms of discrimination against women ( cedaw ). to introduce counselling services. creation of resource material. increase the visibility of icc. encourage student and staff participation in icc collaborating with national and international organizations for collaborative efforts for a gender just campus

text: examinations and evaluation wing plays a pivotal role in systematic determination of assessment, examinations and evaluation of subject ' s merit with reference to teaching learning objectives, its worth and significance by using criteria governed by a set of standards as enunciated in university ordinances. examinations & evaluation wing serves as a custodian of procedures and records. central university of kashmir is offering diverse programmes of study from research degree to certificate programme ( s ) with varying multi - dimensional assessment and evaluation parameters. examinations & evaluation wing is dynamic and adaptive towards such evaluation parameters as amended from time to time with a view to orient student centric practices. reflection - examinations & evaluation, is an attempt to provide an insight about the practices, procedures of the examination and evaluation wing since university journey from 2010. reflection : examinations & evaluation 1 central university of kashmir vision to be a world class institution for creation and dissemination of knowledge to nurture thoughtful, creative, contributing and humane citizens for the society to guide its destiny and for promoting global understanding mission to contribute to the educational, cultural, economic and social advancement of people by providing high quality educational programmes leading to bachelors, masters, professional and doctorate degrees as well as to address the cultural, economic, educational, environmental, health and social needs of the region and nation at large. objectives the central university of kashmir in pursuit of the objectives stipulated in the central universities act of 2009 endeavors : 1. to disseminate and advance knowledge by providing instructional and research facilities in such branches of learning as the university may deem fit ; 2. to make special provisions for integrated courses in humanities, social sciences, science and technology in its educational programmes ; 3. to take appropriate measures for promoting innovations in teaching learning process and inter - disciplinary studies and research ; 4. to educate and train manpower for the development of the country ; 5. to establish linkages with industries for the promotion of science and technology ; and to pay special attention to the improvement of the social and economic conditions and welfare of the people
page_title: cukapi disgenweb in p upload 1665038236055 EEW Reflections pdf

non - cognitive / co - scholastic assessment of behaviour and human development. the examination and evaluation is a formal collection of evidence about student ’ s achievements and growth which involves the planning and instrument construction and the information obtained by techniques of testing, diagnostic and question paper and keeping records, administration, scoring, result compilation and issuance of certification. keeping in view this conceptual backdrop the university is making untiring efforts to implement the robust mechanism by the introduction and encouragement of innovative practices, creative handling of teaching - text: ##w report of academic audit committee - proceedings report of examination discipline committee - proceedings report of post ese workshops - methodology honouring the students - ist university convocation eew - media news 8 introduction central university of kashmir started its academic activities in 2010 by launching three post - graduate programmes viz. masters in english, management studies and information technology. the university later added more under - graduate, post - graduate, integrated and diploma programmes over the years. the university launched law, mathematics, and convergent journalism in the year 2011, education, tourism studies, urdu, economics in 2012, political science, llm in 2013, b. ed in 2014, integrated b. sc. - end semester examination ( ese ) : 60 marks continuous internal assessment ( cia ) : 40 marks there shall be one end semester examination of 3 hours duration carrying 60 % of marks in each course

text: , former professor, department of education, in which lectures were delivered on three important inter - linked topics : assessment, examination, and evaluation. 72 prof. farooq ahmad mir, dean, school of legal studies, while deliberating on the “ assessment of assessments ”, expressed that assessment should not only assess students ’ but also help teachers know about the extent to which students are successfully meeting course learning objectives. the uniform method of assessment is not practically viable, given the uniqueness of each subject. moreover, teachers should have a significant role in the development of assessment methods. the methods should be robust, measurable and should provide just - in - time feedback about the teaching - learning process. prof. farooq ahmad nika, finance officer and director, dic, talked about the examination. he expressed that the examination is a laboratory where the output of the teaching - learning process is tested as per the pre - determined standards in the shape of learning outcomes. we need to follow bloom ’ s taxonomy in letter and spirit so far as cognition, affection, and psychomotor are concerned with developing all the faculties of a student. there need to be some structural reforms in the conduct of the examination. however, the role of the teacher is paramount in making any examination process request oriented. prof. parveen pandit, controller of examination, deliberated on the evaluation. she discussed the historical development of the evaluation system and reflected on its processes, procedures, and characteristics. she expressed that evaluation aims to evaluate student achievements and certificate thereof and help in prognosis / predictions / guidance, among other things. she also talked about the evaluation models ; on practical training for skill acquisition and sound theoretical knowledge serves to equip the students to face real - time challenges and societal needs. the curriculum attempts to address the needs of various stakeholders such as industries, government agencies, banking sector, educational institutions, tourism department, print and electronic media etc. the university gives a great deal of academic freedom enabling the introduction of new & innovative courses. the university with 16 departments has focussed more on skill based technical courses and integrated programmes to ensure employability of the pass outs. university uses the guidelines of regulatory bodies for developing or restructuring the curricula. in addition to the ugc model curriculum, the university is following the guidelines of other regulatory bodies like bar council of india, ncte, tourism hospitality, management associations, etc. in view of the feedback from the stakeholders and the subject experts from across the country, revision of the existing programmes takes place. choice based credit system 1. the university has adopted a very comprehensive scheme of cbcs with a big basket of courses falling under skill enhancement courses, ability enhancement courses and open generic electives. the details are given as under : 2. skill enhancement courses ( sec ) 3. personality development skills ( pds ). 4. entrepreneurship development skill ( eds ). 5. communication skills ( cs ). 6. information and communication technology skills ( ict ). 7. digital photography / image editing ( dp / ie ). cuk / ssr / executive summary 5 ability enhancement course ( aec ) 1. life skill development. 2. peace education. 3. environmental and sustainable development. 4. mental health & adjustment 5. media, peace and conflict studies

text: of social sciences, mumbai. 45. list the teaching methods adopted by the faculty for different programmes : the department of politics & governance appreciates the value of effective curriculum transaction strategies and even an excellent curriculum will not be fruitful if there are no appropriate transaction strategies. we believe that the main responsibility for curriculum transaction lies on the teaching faculty who are encouraged to use innovative and varied types of pedagogies to foster a learning environment for the students where intellectual understanding and critical thinking are nurtured. a broad range of teaching techniques are employed by our teaching faculty to achieve these goals. apart from the traditional lecture method, we also utilize several complementary techniques like seminar ( small group teaching ), tutorials, research seminar, exercise classes, workshops ( classroom based practical classes ), problem - solving sessions, work based practice and fieldwork. information technology aids are used extensively to facilitate these techniques wherever necessary. in order to assess the effectiveness of our curriculum transaction strategies, continuous assessment is done through evaluation and through student feedback. our assessment methods include not only tests and exams, but we also encourage exhibitions, performances, presentations, simulations, multimedia projects, and a variety of other written, oral, and visual methods to evaluate learning outcomes. 46 : no change 47. highlight the participation of students and faculty in extension activities : prof. noor ahmad baba paper presentations and workshops ( 2010 - text: - 2025 lupdated as on 29. 12. 2025 \ 1. definitions a. lammer : jammer is essentially a radio frequency transmitter which is designed to block all radio receivers that fall within its jamming range and covered by frequency bands present in the jamming equipment. b. smart jammer : a jamming equipment which utilizes some intelligence to detect receivers operating in its range and then block that particular receiver, without disturbing other radio services. c. counter drone system : a combination of radar, electro - optics, laser, high power em wave etc., with or without a jamming equipment, to deal with hostile / rogue drones / uavs. d. inland : within the territory of union of india except international border / lac / loc areas. e. defence forces : army, air force, navy and coast guard of the union of india under the ministry of defence. 2, applicability a. as per mha om no. 23014 / 314 / 2013 - vs, dated 10. 03. 2023, any entity intending to manufacture / procure / deploy / use jammer for identification, detection and neutralization of rogue - drones, including jammers as part of counter - rogue drone systems, and use of jammers in jails shall henceforth be required to obtain prior permission from the mha for obtaining a license. in case of jammers for counter - rogue drone operations by the defence forces, the ministry of defence shall be the approving authority. the secretary ( security ), cabinet secretariat will continue to accord prior approval for use of page 1 of 18 jammers / smart jammers for wip / vip security and for deployment of low powered jammers in examination halls. b. accordingly, the subject jammer guidelines is applicable for manufacture / supply / procurement / deployment of jamming equipment / smart jammers for blocking terrestrial and aerial rcieds / telecommunication frequencies for wip / vip security and low powered jammers for deployment in
page_title: www ugc gov in pdfnews 0120812 Advisory Jammer Guidelines 2025 pdf

text: page 1 of 18 jammers / smart jammers for wip / vip security and for deployment of low powered jammers in examination halls. b. accordingly, the subject jammer guidelines is applicable for manufacture / supply / procurement / deployment of jamming equipment / smart jammers for blocking terrestrial and aerial rcieds / telecommunication frequencies for wip / vip security and low powered jammers for deployment in examination halls. 3. norms associated with jammers aa as per department of telecommunications, jammers come under the purview of indian wireless telegraphy act 1933 ( iwta 1933 ) and the act lays down that permission is required for possession / manufacturing and use of jammers. illegal possession of jammers or inviting open tender for procuring / deploying jammers in any manner from any unauthorized manufacturers / suppliers is a violation of the policy of the govt. of india. b. under the foreign trade ( development & regulation ) act 1992, jammers are restricted items and licence is required from dgft for importing jammers into india. dgft grants the licence in consultation with the o / o secretary ( security ), cabinet secretariat. c. central ministries / departments, state governments / union territory administrations, defence forces and central armed police forces ( capfs ), can use jammers from authorized psu vendors only, viz. bharat electronics limited ( bel ) and electronics corporation of india limited ( ecil ), at present, and for the specific purpose for which permission has been granted to them by the o / o secretary ( security ). d. norms for procurement and use of such jammers framed by o / o secretary ( security ), cabinet secretariat, is to be followed in letter and spirit. these norms take into account the need to guard against random proliferation of jammers as well as to ensure that jammers do not unduly interfere with the existing mobile and other telecommunication networks. page 2 of 18 eu private sector organization and / or private individuals
page_title: www ugc gov in pdfnews 0120812 Advisory Jammer Guidelines 2025 pdf

text: procurement and use of such jammers framed by o / o secretary ( security ), cabinet secretariat, is to be followed in letter and spirit. these norms take into account the need to guard against random proliferation of jammers as well as to ensure that jammers do not unduly interfere with the existing mobile and other telecommunication networks. page 2 of 18 eu private sector organization and / or private individuals cannot procure / use / manufacture / import any jamming equipment in india without a valid license. the said license will be given by the nodal ministry / authority concerned with the prior permission of the secretary ( security ). f. any violation in this regard ( manufacturing / possession procurement / usage ) will be dealt as per the law of the land. if any violation is noticed, concerned field office of wpc wing, department of telecommunications, shall take necessary legal action, including seizure / confiscation of the equipment / lodging fir against the holder of such unauthorized jammers with the local police, etc., under intimation to o / o secretary ( security ). g. considering implications of jamming component in counter drone system ( cds ) / standalone jammers on security of wips / vips, information on installation / deployment of all kind of rf jamming solutions need to be maintained by the nodal ministry / authority responsible for such jammers / cds and shared by the regulating authority with the o / o secretary ( security ), spg, ib and other security agencies concerned. 4. norms for manufacture / procurement / deployment / use of jammers by ministries / departments, state governments / union territory administrations, defence forces and central armed police forces ( capfs ) a. secretary ( security ), cabinet secretariat, is i ( a / b / c ) and annexure - ii. c. jammers can be procured only by central ministries / departments, state governments / union territory administrations, defence forces, central armed page 3 of 18 police forces ( capfs ) and other security agencies / organizations under the administrative control of central and state government / uts. d. defence forces ( army / air force / navy / coast guard ) are kept out of the purview of jammer guidelines for military use / military grade jammers or jammers to be deployed for defence / security purposes at international borders / lac / loc which are to be regulated as per appropriate guidelines of the mod. however, if a jammer is required to be deployed at defence force headquarters / other inland defence site / campus, for testing or any other purpose, prior concurrence is required to be taken from the office of secretary ( security ), cabinet secretariat, as per the procedure laid down in these guidelines. e. jammers procured / deployed by defence forces for inland operational and administrative purposes such as in conference halls / ops room / examination halls etc., and procurement and deployment of vehicle mounted / portable rcied jammers etc. for convoy movements in inland areas, will be governed by the jammer guidelines. f. the permission for manufacture / procurement / deployment / use of jammers is granted in consultation with security agencies who maintain a database of available jammers. at present jammer models offered by m / s electronics corporation of india ( ecil )

text: and procurement and deployment of vehicle mounted / portable rcied jammers etc. for convoy movements in inland areas, will be governed by the jammer guidelines. f. the permission for manufacture / procurement / deployment / use of jammers is granted in consultation with security agencies who maintain a database of available jammers. at present jammer models offered by m / s electronics corporation of india ( ecil ) & m / s bharat electronics limited ( bel ) can only be procured. other central / state psus, desirous of becoming approved vendor of jammers, may submit their proposal to the o / o secretary ( security ), cabinet secretariat, as per the procedure laid down in section 6 of the jammer guidelines. g a jammer models offered by authorized vendors are first evaluated by a board of officers ( boo ). the boo, constituted with the approval of secretary ( security ), consists of officers from security agencies, o / o wireless advisor ( department of telecommunication ) and drdo, and such other members as may be approved by the secretary ( security ). boo may seek permission of secretary ( security ) for co - opting other members / experts in the relevant field, page 4 of 18 as per the requirement of the technical evaluation of the equipment concerned. h. only those models can be procured which have been found suitable by the board and approved by the secretary ( security ). i. any jammer model declared obsolete / upgradable in terms of technology / frequency band coverage by b. o. o. and approved by secretary ( security ), is to be suitably upgraded or replaced by the user agencies with advanced models from time to time, to meet the desired purpose of security. list of such models ( approved / obsolete / upgradable ) will be shared with all the stakeholders by the o / o secretary ( security ). j. inviting open tender or procuring / deploying / demonstrating jammers in any manner from unauthorized manufacturers is a

text: be suitably upgraded or replaced by the user agencies with advanced models from time to time, to meet the desired purpose of security. list of such models ( approved / obsolete / upgradable ) will be shared with all the stakeholders by the o / o secretary ( security ). j. inviting open tender or procuring / deploying / demonstrating jammers in any manner from unauthorized manufacturers is a violation of the policy of government of india and will be dealt with as per the law of the land. k. statutory / govt. recognized examination conducting bodies are also allowed to deploy low powered / classroom jammers, on lease basis, as per the norms and procedure laid down in section 5 of the jammer guidelines. i. in case of movement of spg protectee ( s ) in the vicinity, all types of jammers procured by government agencies should be deployed in consultation with spg m. the procuring agency will not re - sell or transfer the jammer to another agency without clearance / noc from the o / o secretary ( security ). 5. norms for statutory / govt. recognized examination conducting bodies desirous of using jammers in examination centres ag the statutory / govt. recognized examination conducting bodies are allowed to deploy low powered jammers / classroom jammers to prevent cheating and other malpractices during examinations, by leasing the jammers through the empanelled psus viz., m / s bel / m / s ecil. examination conducting bodies have to pay only for using jammers on the specific date of examination. page 5 of 18 b. only approved jammer models ( annexure - iii ) can be deployed by examination conducting bodies for this purpose. while deploying the jammers it will be ensured that the jammers do not interfere with existing mobile communication network outside examination centres. each jammer deployed at the examination centre will be accounted for and any discrepancy in this regard will be reported immediately to the appropriate law enforcement agency

iv. 6. procedure for registration of psu vendors ag a central or state government undertaking, with requisite manufacturing capability and necessary technology, can request for registration as a jammer manufacturer / vendor. it can furnish its proposal to the office of secretary ( security ), cabinet secretariat, giving details regarding specifications of the equipment offered, jamming technology, source of technology, manufacturing capacity, financial strength, ability to deploy jammers on pan india basis, ability to provide after sales service, ability to continuously upgrade jamming technology, etc. b. the application would be processed in consultation with the security agencies and ministries / departments concerned, including the administrative ministry of the central psu / state govt, concerned. the empanelment of jammer models offered by the psu will be subject to prior evaluation by the boo. 7. instructions for authorized vendors a. the authorized vendors will develop technology, in a timely manner, to jam communications of present and upcoming technologies / futuristic communication bands, to counter threats associated with radio frequency communication. b. global technologies in field of jammers may be explored by authorized vendors and best international practices followed and adopted / implemented by them in designing jammer systems so as to provide updated and effective jarnrning systems. page 7 of 18 8. procedure for disposal of condemned / non - text: calendar year, the proposal will be considered by the office of secretary ( security ) only if submitted through the respective competent authority, who had initially nominated the nodal officer. 7. all the examination conducting bodies are therefore advised to take note of the above instructions for submitting proposals to this office and ensure compliance. # # # # # # # page 16 of 18 annexure - v sop for condemnation and disposal of jammers a. the responsibility to dispose a jammer system which has been declared condemned / non - operational / unserviceable / obsolete lies with the user department / security agency ( states / ut / defence / capf etc. ). b. the portable jammers may be returned on ' as is where is ’ basis and in vehicle mounted jammers, only the jammer portion ( exciter, power amplifier, control card, antenna ) along with related accessories ( cpu, rcu, power supplies, rf cables ) may be returned to m / s bel / m / s ecil. assistance of m / s bel / m / s ecil for dismantling of vehicle mounted jammer may be taken by user department / agency, if required. cg in all procurement cases, appropriate terms and conditions for dismantling and disposal of jammer system may be incorporated in the contract / agreement signed between user agency and the jammer vendor. the old cases may be dealt by the user department / security agency ( states / ut / defence / capf etc. ) with m / s bel / m / s ecil on mutual agreement basis. d. advised prescribed life of vehicle mounted jammers is 10 years from the date of manufacture, subject to technical inspection and review by the respective psu ( m / s bel / m / s ecil ) during the 7th year, for its continued operability. e. advised prescribed life of portable jammers is 7 years, from the date of manufacture. the life may be extended to 10 years

text: d. advised prescribed life of vehicle mounted jammers is 10 years from the date of manufacture, subject to technical inspection and review by the respective psu ( m / s bel / m / s ecil ) during the 7th year, for its continued operability. e. advised prescribed life of portable jammers is 7 years, from the date of manufacture. the life may be extended to 10 years subject to the technical inspection by the respective psu ( m / s bel / m / s ecil ) factoring in the availability of the upgraded electronic components pertaining to that jammer. f. permission by user department / security agency ( states / ut / defence / capf etc. ) from o / o secretary ( security ) for condemnation and disposal of jammers is not required. however, o / o secretary ( security ) needs to be kept informed of condemnation and disposal. page 17 of 18

text: prof. manish r. joshi secretary f. no. 193 - 3 / 2025 ( ic ) public notice call for nominations - unesco - hamdan prize for teacher development ( 2025 - 2026 edition ) unesco has invited nominations for the 2025 - 2026 edition of the unesco - hamdan prize for teacher development, established in 2008 and funded by the hamdan bin rashid al maktoum foundation, united arab emirates. the prize aims to promote teacher education and pro fessional development with a view to improving teaching practices worldwide, especially in developing countries and marginalized communities. for the 2025 - 2026 edition, three laureates will be selected, each receiving an award of usd 100, 000. eligible nominees include institutions, organizations, and educational or research institutes that have made outstanding contributions to enhancing the quality of teaching and learning. university grants commission ( ministry of education, govt. of indio ) applications are to be submitted in english or french through the dedicated online platform latest by 31 october 2025 ( midnight, paris time, utc + 1 ), each government may endorse up to five nominees. to all the heis. g 6, 1947 / 28 " august, 2025 accordingly, interested and eligible institutions / organizations are requested to submit their applications complcte in all respect to the ministry ofeducation, department of higher education at the following address latest by 30h september, 2025 for further scrutiny and sending the final nominations to unesco by the stipulated time ( 31 october, 2025 ) : ms. madhu bala soni room no. 203 - a c ' wing under secretary to the government of india ministry of education, department of higher education dr. rajendra prasad road shastri bhavan, new delhi - 110001 contact no. : + 91 - 011 - 2338 4442, e - mail : inc. edu @ nic. in further details about the prize and the selection process are available at the website link

text: prof. manish r. joshi secretary f. no. 1 - 9 / 2023 ( equivalence ) public notice university grants commission ( ministry of education, govt. of india ) 14 da 1947 / 04 april, 2025 university grants commission ( recognition and grant of equivalence to qualifications obtained from foreign educational institutions ) regulations, 2025 in the context of the national education policy ( nep ), 2020, university grants commission has enumerated a wide array of activities such as internationally relevant curricula, the brand building of indian educational institutions abroad, academic and research collaboration with foreign universities, credit recognition under twinning arrangements, a global citizenship approach, engaging with foreign alumni and setting up and operation of campuses of foreign higher educational institutions in india. further, the university grants commission ( ugc ) has issued the regulations on grant of equivalence to qualifications obtained from foreign higher education institutions, 2025. stakeholders can now apply for equivalence of their foreign academic qualifications through the ugc equivalence portal ( https : / / equivalence. ugc. ac. in ). the detailed process is outlined in the 2025 regulations, available on ugc website. aeigiate u hi, e fotr - 110002 | bahadur shah zafar marg, new delhi - 110002 ghtt ( ph ) : 011 - 23236288 / 23239337 | qua qa ( - ) ( e - mail ) : secyug @ nic. in ( nfaq yft )

text: selection of indian candidates for study in the russian federation for the 2026 / 27 academic year the government of the russian federation annually provides scholarships for indian citizens to study at russian educational institutions. this scholarship program covers almost all universities in russia – from moscow and st. petersburg to krasnodar, kaliningrad and vladivostok – with the exception of lomonosov moscow state university, mgimo. students can apply for all levels o f education : bachelor ' s, specialist ’ s, master ' s and phd programs, advanced training courses. all courses are available ( medicine, pharmacy, engineering, architecture, agriculture, management, economics, humanities and social sciences, mathematics, space, aviation, sports, art, etc. ). a number of programs, in particular in the field of medicine, are taught in english. proficiency in russian is not required. if students don ’ t speak russian, they can simply select an additional option in their application about the need to learn russian. in this case, they will study russian for one year. after that, they will be able to move on to studying their chosen course. all applications for scholarships of the russian government are submitted via the website https : / / www. education - in - russia. com. it is not possible to submit applications in any other way. students do not have to pass tests and exams. the assessment of the dossier consists of two assessments : - the average grade for the diploma of the previous education ; - a portfolio ( scientific publications ; a letter of recommendation signed by the rector or vice - rector of the russian university chosen by the candidate ; certificates of the winner of international or national competitions, olympiads ). students can choose up to 6 universities in the application in priority order. but they will be enrolled to the university of the six that chooses them. the selection is carried out in two stages. the first stage ends on january 15. at this stage, the submitted documents are checked, on the

text: women thought leaders51 bharatiya vidvat parishat ( bvp ) bharatiya vidvat parishat ©vp ) is a trust dedicated to promoting research and studies in indian shastras and addressing cultural and social issues of contemporary relevance. estab - lished in 2009 as a google mailing group, it has evolved into a dynamic academic community of over 2, 500 scholars, researchers, and educators from across the world. from its inception, bvp has sought to create a free, open, and collaborative intellectual space where scholars can share insights, research, and perspectives rooted in indian knowledge systems while engaging with the needs of the modern world. it serves as a bridge between traditional scholarship and present - day inquiry, fostering dialogue that is both philosophically deep and socially relevant. bvp has organized numerous landmark programs such as the “ dispassionate churning of indology ” conference, which brought togeth - er leading scholars from around the globe. as a pioneer of women ’ s empowerment in academic discourse, bvp successfully conducted the mahila vakyartha sabha, creating space for women ’ s voices in the realm of shastra studies. the organization also convened the prestigious all india oriental conference ( aioc ) in 2024, marking a milestone in its journey of scholarly excellence. standing at the confluence of qfa ( timeless wisdom ) and ph ( creative action ), bvp contin - ucs to uphold india ’ s rich intellectual traditions while inspiring new thought, innovation, and collective growth. it remains a living platform where india ’ s ancient wisdom meets her contem - pc ) rar ) ' asprraaons. sharanya - - tbe flagbearer of social llpliftmekt established on august 6, 2016, sharanya stands as a beacon of social transformation dedicated to empowering marginalized sections of society through education, self - reliance, and cultural resurgence. more than an organization,

text: i ( { - i @ ) ( e - mail ) : secy. ugc @ nic. in stipendium hungaricum scholarship programme introduction - as per the provisions mentioned in the educational exchange programme agreement signed between the ministry of human capacities of hungary and the ministry of education, govt. of india, the hungarian govt. has offered scholarships for 200 indian students to pursue undergraduate, postgraduate and doctoral studies in hungary. scholarship details are as follows : * ug - 50 scholarships * pg - 75 scholarships * phd - 30 scholarships * interpreter, translator and literary translator studies - 5 scholarships postgraduate courses and research – 40 scholarships ( part time ) * all these programmes will be full time eligibility : - 1. must be an indian citizen. 2. applicants born after 31st august, 2008 shall not be considered. 3. must possess at least 60 % in the last examination passed. 4. must have completed his / her last study programme from a recognized indian educational institute only. however, students who have availed stipendium hungaricum scholarship may apply provided they apply for a different / higher level of study programme. 5. must have applied online to tempus public foundation. note : - an application shall be considered by the ugc only when the candidate applies online by following appropriate procedure. incomplete application, application without relevant documents and application received after du e date shall not be considered. before applying, the applicant should ascertain that the programme applied is open for indian students. application for stipendium hungaricum scholarship programme 1. applicant id : photograph 2. name : ( latest ) 3. father ’ s name : 4. mother ’ s name : 5. gender : 6. date of birth : 7. nationality : 8. proof of indian citizenship : ( passport no. / any other id ) 9. residential address : pin code : for communication ( complete ) 10. mobile no. : 11. email id. text: ll ' r - - dr. archana thakur joint secretary f. no. i 93 - 3 / 2024 ( 1c ) public notice fct4t61tli ( illl ' 3ww1 ' university grants commission ( rer ' ' lffiff mcm ) ( ministry of education, govt. of india ) llm lfllf, - 110002 bahadur shah zafar marg, new delhi - 110002 phone : off : 0l l - 23239597 ' t.. : ¾ " e - mail : archana. ugc @ nic. in 21st march, 2025 call for nominations for the 2025 unesco - japan prize on education for sustainable development nominations are invited for the eighth edition of the unesco japan prize on education for sustainable development. funded by the government of japan, the prize was established by the unesco executive board in 20 i 4 to reward exceptional projects and programmes undertaken in the field of education for sustainable development. the three laureates of the 2025 prize will receive an award of us $ 50, 000 each. in addition, winners will be invited to join the esd for 2030 global network ( esd - net 2030 ), in order to establish long term collaboration with unesco beyond the award ceremony. the nominations should be submitted in english or french, by midnight on 18 april, 2025 ( paris time, utc + i ) via an online platform at the link provided. unesco encourages member states to implement a national selection process and to publicly announce the selected nominees. further information on the prize, use of on line platform and the nomination process can be found at https : / / en. unesco. org / prize - esd. an explanatory note, giving details of establishment of prize, nominations, eligibility and selection criteria, submission procedure, nomination form, etc as well as the statutes of the prize are also attached below. all the hels are requested to forward nomination to

text: , use of on line platform and the nomination process can be found at https : / / en. unesco. org / prize - esd. an explanatory note, giving details of establishment of prize, nominations, eligibility and selection criteria, submission procedure, nomination form, etc as well as the statutes of the prize are also attached below. all the hels are requested to forward nomination to the section officer ( inc ) ministry of education, government of india, shastri bhawan, new delhi and email to : - inc. edu nic. in not later than 26th march, 2025. i t ( dr. archana thakur ) encl. : as above. unesco 7, place de fontenoy 75352 paris 07 sp, france ref. : cl / 4497 subject : call for nominations for the 2025 unesco - japan prize on education for sustainable development dear minister, i am pleased to invite your government to submit nominations for the eighth edition of the unesco - japan prize on education for sustainable development. in the face of climate disruption and threats to biodiversity, it is more important than ever to give greater prominence to education for sustainable development in school programmes. funded by the government of japan, the prize was established by the unesco executive board in 2014 to reward exceptional projects and programmes undertaken in the field of education for sustainable development. the three laureates of the 2025 prize will receive an award of us $ 50, 000 each. in addition, winners will be invited to join the esd for 2030 global network ( esd net 2030 ), in order to establish long - term collaboration with unesco beyond the award ceremony. nominations should be submitted in english or french, by midnight on 18 april 2025 ( paris time, utc + 1 ), via an online platform at the following link : https : / / unesco. sharepoint. com / sites / committees / esd - prize /. each government may present up to three nominees
